ip_outcome_note: Confirmed via PokemonTCG.io swsh2-29 card data. National Pokedex no. 126 matches. Art description (orange-red bipedal duck-billed fire creature, volcanic setting) is canonical Magmar design.

trivia_bullets.text: Magmar appears in Sword & Shield — Rebel Clash (2020), printed as card no. 029/192 directly adjacent to its counterpart Electabuzz (058/192) — both commons in the same set, a deliberate production pairing.

Magmar (National Pokedex no. 126) and Electabuzz (no. 125) are the most symmetrical version-exclusive pair in Generation I. Magmar was Blue-exclusive, Electabuzz Red-exclusive. Pre-evolutions added in Gen II, final evolutions in Gen IV via trade+item (Magmarizer/Electirizer). Step-for-step generation parity across three stages. Note: the existing Connections section had version exclusivity reversed (Red/Blue) — corrected to Blue/Red per Bulbapedia.
sources: WebSearch: TheGamer.com 'Magmar Versus Electabuzz Is Secretly Pokemon's Best Story'

trivia_bullets.text: "Its entire body is burning. When it breathes, the temperature rises. When it sneezes, flames shoot out!"

related_cards.note: Gen IV final evolution via Magmarizer trade
